corridor_classification_notes_ut_moody_c: **Rainey Street + Rainey-adjacent high-rise residential towers (~ south shore / East-side lakefront canyon):** extreme caution for NEW outdoor amplified + late-hours bass; HOA / residential complaint vectors differ from nightlife-zoned corridors even when physically proximate to hotels.

**Red River Cultural District + 6th & Neches / Red River intersections:** geographically NOT Rainey. Venues referencing **510 / 512 Neches** sit squarely in Old Sixth / Entertainment overlay logic — amplified entertainment tolerance historically higher than lakeside canyon towers (**still site-specific entitlement work** — not blanket approval).

**CBD Class-A podium retail (approx. Frost Bank Tower / Austin Tower / Endeavor-listed Congress bases):** structurally analogous to rejected **100 / 501 Congress** skyscraper pedestal retail archetypes — penalize landlord CAM friction + roof rights unknowability absent explicit exceptions.
mislabel_warnings: **510 Neches ≠ Rainey-orbit attribution.** Zo `maps_search` corroborates 510 Neches lies ~north/west relative to labelled Rainey Street centroid; classify as **Sixth/Neches / Old Sixth / RR-adjacent** unless future survey says otherwise.

Urban marketing PDFs referencing “Rainey Street District” branding for **44 East Ave** denote **tower podium / condo retail strata** (`condo_policy=reject` baseline) regardless of directional marketing copy.

receipts_notes: Texas Comptroller publishes obligation-level filings on Texas Open Data: dataset `naix-2893` Mixed Beverage Gross Receipts (https://data.texas.gov/resource/naix-2893.json). Rows include liquor/wine/beer/cover buckets but **omit explicit food-sales columns** verified 2026-05-13; food-vs-alcohol margin requires alternate exports or CFO modeling. Receipt pulls this run archived under cities/austin/data/receipts/*.json + receipt-analysis-notes-2026-05-13.md.
